packet.criterion_text: Objective severity trend: PASI, BSA, and/or IGA show a directional change across visits.

evidence.quote: PASI 6.1, BSA 8%, IGA 2 (mild-to-moderate plaque elevation and scale).
PASI 6.3 today (baseline 6.1 on 2026-02-02 — essentially flat, within measurement noise). BSA 8%. IGA 2. DLQI 8, reflecting ongoing nocturnal itch and mild sleep disturbance.

packet.reasoning: Across two dated encounters (2026-02-02 and 2026-03-16), PASI moved from 6.1 to 6.3 — a rise of only 0.2 points, which the chart itself characterizes as 'essentially flat, within measurement noise.' BSA remained unchanged at 8% and IGA remained unchanged at 2 at both visits. The rubric requires a clinically meaningful increase (PASI rise ≥ 2 points or IGA step-up) to establish worsening; neither threshold is met. No improving trend is documented either. The objective indices are stable across the two available encounters.

packet.criterion_text: Symptom trajectory, including nail and joint involvement, shows a directional change.
packet.reasoning: No candidates were provided for this criterion. The chart search returned no verbatim quotes from any document (visit1_derm_note, visit2_derm_note, or visit2_med_list) addressing symptom trajectory, nail findings (pitting, onycholysis), joint pain, new plaque sites, or scalp involvement. Per Rule 3, with empty candidates that do not speak to the criterion, status must be 'insufficient.' The rubric notes that absence of mention is not evidence of absence, but without any candidate quotes there is no basis to characterize a directional change.

packet.criterion_text: DLQI (Dermatology Life Quality Index) trend shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: DLQI 7, driven by itch, clothing choices, and self-consciousness during exercise.
DLQI 8, reflecting ongoing nocturnal itch and mild sleep disturbance.

packet.reasoning: DLQI increased from 7 (2026-02-02) to 8 (2026-03-16), a rise of 1 point. The rubric requires an increase of ≥ 4 points or a sustained upward trend across more than two data points to establish worsening. A single-interval rise of 1 point does not meet the worsening threshold. Only two data points are available, so a 'sustained upward trend' cannot be confirmed. The DLQI is therefore stable across the two documented encounters.

packet.criterion_text: Treatment-response adequacy: the current regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial.
packet.reasoning: No candidates were provided for this criterion. The chart search returned no verbatim quotes from any available document identifying the current regimen, its start date, dosing schedule, or adherence record. Without this information it is impossible to determine whether the regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial of sufficient duration. Per Rule 3, status is 'insufficient.'

packet.criterion_text: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.
packet.reasoning: No candidates were provided for this criterion. The chart search returned no verbatim quotes addressing any confounder class (missed or stopped medications, intercurrent infection, recent steroid taper or withdrawal, or seasonal pattern). Per Rule 4, when objective indices worsen the assembler attributes worsening to disease activity and sets status 'worsening' as the affirmative best-case position; whether the absence of confounder documentation defeats this attribution is reserved for adversarial review. Note that the objective indices in criterion 1 are actually stable rather than worsening, which limits the practical force of this attribution, but the criterion is evaluated on its own terms: no confounder evidence was found to rebut a disease-activity attribution. [No verifiable chart evidence survived verification for this criterion, so its status cannot be asserted.]
